_meta.contract_ref: CONTRACT.md §5 -- key debates, not laundry list; each quantified; evidence_quality graded honestly; bull stays within addressable ceiling
_meta.note_on_bull_px: Daedalus's stated bull segment EVs (EDA $91.5B + IP $20.5B + Ansys $32.0B - $7.52B net debt / 0.1914B shares) arithmetically imply ~$713/share; Daedalus's reported $624 is the conservative bound used here and reflects a more restrained interim FCF treatment. Both figures clear the $455.51 current price. Boreas adopts $624 as the ceiling-bounded bull, consistent with CONTRACT §4 direction that the bull may not breach the addressable ceiling.

bull_thesis: SNPS is a software duopoly priced as if the Ansys acquisition delivers nothing beyond Ansys's standalone growth and AI-driven EDA demand adds zero pricing power -- a 'zero optionality' scenario embedded in the current 26x FY2027 P/E. The bull reads the same evidence differently on one decisive variable: whether agentic AI consumption pricing breaks the 13-15% historical ceiling on EDA/IP/Sim wallet-share of semiconductor R&D. The mechanism is not incremental -- it is structural. Agent Engineer (20 customers evaluating 25+ specialized agents at Converge 2026) invokes licensed tool compute orders of magnitude more intensively than a human engineer on a seat license. If SNPS captures even half the implied value uplift through a subscription-plus-consumption model, the per-customer revenue envelope doubles without requiring a new customer or a market share gain. Three levers compound this: (1) EDA revenue at FY2030 reaches $8.80B vs the base's $7.09B as wallet-share expands from 13.5% to 18%; (2) Design IP revenue reaches $4.30B vs $3.38B as the royalty model transition succeeds and hyperscaler custom silicon volumes (Google TPU, AWS Trainium, Meta MTIA, all confirmed active SNPS EDA customers) generate per-chip recurring revenue from FY2028 onward; (3) Ansys reaches $5.10B vs $4.25B as Multiphysics Fusion -- the only chip-to-system multiphysics signoff product GA as of June 17 2026 with no competitor -- converts AI hardware design complexity into cross-sell revenue from existing SNPS EDA customers. Combined, these three shifts lift the bull SOTP to $624/share against a current price of $455.51, a gap the market has not closed because no sell-side house has modeled consumption revenue at scale in its base case.
crux_reading: The base reads wallet-share expanding from 13.5% to 15% by FY2030 -- the upper end of the 20-year historical range, driven primarily by market growth with modest consumption contribution. The bull reads the agentic AI pricing transition as a ceiling-break event, not a ceiling-approach: AI agents running 24/7 invoke tool licenses 3-5x more intensively than human engineers working 8-hour shifts. If even 30-50% of that value uplift is captured in the consumption tier, the per-project revenue from an AI-augmented design team exceeds the historical per-seat equivalent. The critical distinction from the base: this is a billing-model change, not a market-share change. SNPS does not need to win customers from Cadence to drive the bull -- it needs existing customers (who already have subscriptions) to accept a metered consumption tier on top. The historical precedent is cloud computing (which broke the IT capex budget ceiling) and SaaS (which broke per-seat models) -- in both cases new billing visibility unlocked spending that had been unmetered.

scenario_spec.ceiling_check: Bull FY2030 revenue $18.2B vs combined EDA+IP+Sim TAM growing to ~$42B at 13% bull CAGR. SNPS implied capture 43%. Current 31%. Ceiling passes only if agentic consumption revenue and IP royalties are ADDITIVE to the existing TAM -- they are new billing categories, not redistribution. Theia flags this as AT CEILING; Boreas treats it as the honest upper bound, not a target.
scenario_spec.note: Daedalus's stated segment EVs sum to $144B, implying ~$713/share from the arithmetic; Daedalus's reported $624 reflects a more conservative interim FCF treatment. Boreas uses $624 as the binding ceiling per CONTRACT §4.

key_debates.title: Agentic AI breaks the wallet-share ceiling: consumption pricing adds a new revenue layer to an unchanged customer base
key_debates.bull_view: For 20+ years EDA has been 13-15% of chip design R&D spend -- the ceiling has held because EDA tools became more efficient, offsetting the growing complexity per design. Agentic AI breaks this dynamic through a specific mechanism that has no historical precedent in EDA: AI agents running 24/7 invoke licensed tool compute 3-5x more per design cycle than a human engineer running the same tools 8 hours per day. SNPS's Agent Engineer (L4 multi-agent stack, 25+ specialized agents) already has 20 customers in active evaluation as of Converge, March 2026. Management guided the first incremental consumption revenue in FY2027. The consumption model charges on top of the existing subscription -- meaning SNPS earns on the same customer account at 1.5-2.5x the prior rate without winning a single new customer. The bull scenario requires $500M+ of agentic consumption revenue by FY2030 -- approximately 4% of the combined EDA market. With SNPS holding 31% EDA share, this is plausible if 10-15% of SNPS's 2,000+ EDA customers adopt the consumption tier by FY2029. No sell-side house has modeled this in its base case (GS pre-earnings May 2026 note, Citi March 2026 IR meeting notes both classify it as 'FY2027+ optionality'). The EDA segment in the bull reaches $8.80B at FY2030 at 52% non-GAAP margin, NOPAT of $4.03B, exit multiple of 29.5x (a consumption premium above the 22.4x Gordon base, justified because a metered recurring consumption stream is worth more per dollar of NOPAT than a flat subscription). EV of $91.5B vs base $49.3B. This debate is the crux: it drives $160/share of the $233 base-to-bull spread.
what_must_be_true: At least 10-15% of SNPS's 2,000+ EDA customers adopt the consumption tier by FY2029, generating $500M+ incremental annual revenue -- the 20 evaluation customers at Converge 2026 are the leading indicator

Customers accept a hybrid subscription-plus-consumption billing model; hyperscalers (Google, AWS, Meta, Microsoft -- all confirmed active SNPS EDA customers via Pheme capex data) agree to metered consumption despite their preference for predictable cost structures
Agent Engineer invokes licensed tool compute (not open-weight models or around-license agent frameworks) -- the billing dependency on SNPS's EDA infrastructure must be embedded, not circumventable
September 30 Investor Day reveals a formal consumption pricing structure with customer adoption metrics; this is the first de-risking event for this lever
key_debates.dollar_per_share_above_base: $160/share above the $390.86 base (proportional share of the $233 base-to-$624 bull spread; the dominant lever at 69% of total bull uplift). Stated differently: EDA segment EV moves from $49.3B (base) to $91.5B (bull) = $42.2B incremental, or $221/share at 0.1914B shares -- the $160 figure reflects Daedalus's conservative interim FCF treatment on the $624 ceiling.
key_debates.evidence_quality_basis: Agent Engineer's 20 evaluation customers and management's FY2027 first-revenue guidance are real (sourced: SNPS Converge March 2026; Futurum Group Q2 FY2026 analysis). The consumption model architecture (subscription-plus-consumption) is confirmed. The dollar contribution at scale is extrapolated -- no booking, no disclosed contract size, no consumption revenue recognized yet. This is not HOPED-FOR (there is a real product in real evaluations); it is EXTRAPOLATED (a reasonable read of a visible trend, not yet booked).
key_debates.what_changes_my_mind: Three or more quarters pass after FY2027 guidance is set with no consumption revenue line item or separate disclosure. Alternatively: one of the top-5 hyperscaler EDA customers publicly negotiates a fixed-fee ELA that explicitly caps consumption charges -- that signals customer resistance, not adoption. The monitor variable is the September 30 Investor Day and the Q4 FY2026 / Q1 FY2027 earnings calls for first revenue contribution commentary.

key_debates.title: Hyperscaler IP royalty model transforms a cyclical revenue stream into a per-chip recurring annuity from FY2028
key_debates.bull_view: SNPS holds 55% share of wired interface IP -- UCIe, HBM, PCIe, LPDDR -- the chokepoint for every advanced AI chip using chiplet or multi-die architecture. Every NVIDIA GB200/GB300 CoWoS package, every AMD Instinct MI350, every hyperscaler custom ASIC (Google TPU v5, AWS Trainium 2, Microsoft Maia, Meta MTIA) that uses HBM3E or UCIe interconnects embeds SNPS IP. Management guided the royalty model transition ('near end FY26') moving these accounts from upfront license fees to per-chip royalties. The math: at hyperscaler AI chip volumes, even $0.05-0.10 per chip on 1 billion+ inference ASICs per year = $50-100M per hyperscaler. Across Google, AWS, Microsoft, Meta (four named companies with confirmed custom silicon programs), $200-400M of recurring IP royalty revenue by FY2030 is plausible. SNPS launched PCIe 7.0 IP in June 2026 (industry-first per SNPS; source: Pheme bundle). Samsung 2nm and 3D-IC collaboration announced June 2026 adds another design program that will require SNPS IP. The IP segment in the bull reaches $4.30B at FY2030 at 38% margin, NOPAT $1.44B, exit multiple 18x (Gordon-consistent; royalty streams carry near-pure-profit incremental margin, justifying the 18x vs base 17.6x). The FY2027 near-term headwind from royalty model transition (upfront revenue deferred to per-chip timing) is real and embedded in the bull path -- IP may show a $100-200M revenue headwind in FY2027 before the royalty wave arrives FY2028-FY2030 (Theia R5). This debate contributes $32/share above base.
what_must_be_true: At least three of the four named hyperscalers (Google, AWS, Microsoft, Meta) accept royalty-bearing IP license terms by end FY2027; hyperscalers have historically resisted ARM-style royalty models, but SNPS's 55% chokepoint share in wired interface IP gives it structural leverage on PCIe, UCIe, and HBM where alternatives are thinner
The per-chip royalty rate exceeds the NPV of forgone upfront license revenue; management has implicitly committed to this by guiding the transition
Chip design cycles complete on schedule: designs starting late FY2026 tape out by late FY2027 and go to volume production by FY2028, triggering royalty recognition

No emergence of an alternative wired interface IP provider that breaks SNPS's 55% share in the 1-2 year horizon before royalty volumes ramp (UCIe is an open standard, but SNPS is a founding member with the most mature PHY implementation)
key_debates.dollar_per_share_above_base: $32/share above the $390.86 base (proportional share: IP segment EV moves from $12.1B to $20.5B = $8.4B incremental / 0.1914B shares = $44/share; the $32 is the Daedalus-ceiling-consistent figure). Source: IP bull NOPAT of $1.44B x 18x exit multiple = $25.9B EV at T=FY2030, discounted at IP WACC 9.1% over 4 years.
key_debates.evidence_quality_basis: The royalty model transition is CONTRACTED in the sense that management has publicly guided it (GS May 2026, Citi March 2026 IR meeting notes); the 55% wired interface IP share is documented (Theia; multiple sources). The royalty revenue itself is EXTRAPOLATED -- per-chip rates and volume assumptions are reasonable extrapolations from the scale of confirmed hyperscaler custom silicon programs but no royalty contract terms are disclosed. SNPS's accounting policy specifically excludes future royalty payments from the reported $11B backlog (10-Q note), which means royalty upside is genuinely incremental and NOT already counted in the contracted base.
key_debates.what_changes_my_mind: Amazon or Google publicly announces a competing wired interface IP source or a successful in-house UCIe PHY implementation that bypasses SNPS licensing. Alternatively: management commentary in Q4 FY2026 or Q1 FY2027 earnings that the royalty model transition is being 'deferred' or that hyperscaler resistance is stronger than expected. The monitor variable is FY2027 Design IP QoQ growth trajectory -- Theia's monitoring variable is Design IP sequential growth in 2H FY2026; sustained QoQ decline after the transition would signal the royalty hole is deeper than the base expects.

key_debates.title: Multiphysics Fusion converts AI hardware complexity from a design burden into a SNPS cross-sell opportunity with no competitive response
key_debates.bull_view: Multiphysics Fusion went GA on June 17 2026 as the only chip-to-system product integrating EDA timing signoff with multiphysics thermal and electromagnetic analysis. The target customer base is every designer of advanced AI hardware using chiplet or 3D IC architecture -- which is, in 2026, nearly every high-value chip program. TSMC CoWoS capacity (the primary multi-die packaging technology for NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom AI hardware) is running at ~50,000 wafers/month with multiple concurrent customer programs. Each CoWoS-based chip program faces the same challenge: manual thermal and EM workflows do not scale to multi-die complexity, and missed signoff requirements at tape-out cause schedule slips and yield loss worth hundreds of millions. Multiphysics Fusion addresses this directly. Beta customers are described as 'market leaders' (SNPS GA announcement, HPCwire June 2026) -- not named, but the description narrows to NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom, and hyperscalers with active multi-die programs. The cross-sell motion is structurally advantaged: SNPS's EDA sales team already has the champion relationship with IC design teams that buy EDA tools. Simulation buyers (systems engineers) are a different persona, but Multiphysics Fusion deliberately bridges the gap -- it runs within the EDA environment, reducing the procurement friction of adopting a standalone Ansys tool. At $5-10M per program per year and 20-30 active AI hardware programs using CoWoS or equivalent, the incremental Ansys cross-sell revenue from Multiphysics Fusion alone is $100-300M by FY2028. Added to Ansys's base automotive and aerospace growth, the segment reaches $5.10B at FY2030 at 42% margin -- NOPAT $1.89B, exit 22x (synergy delivery confirmed, removing the 0.8x integration risk discount from the base's 19.5x). Ansys segment EV moves from $21.0B to $32.0B, contributing $42/share to the bull.
what_must_be_true: At least 10-20 AI hardware programs formally adopt Multiphysics Fusion by end FY2027; the GA launch (June 2026) with confirmed beta 'market leaders' means the evaluation cycle is already running -- the first named customer win in earnings commentary is the key trigger

The EDA-to-simulation cross-sell motion executes: SNPS's EDA sales team must bring Multiphysics Fusion to the same conversation as EDA renewal, rather than having it compete as a standalone simulation tool against Ansys's own direct sales team (which serves different contacts)

No competitor launches a comparable chip-to-system multiphysics EDA integration in the 12-18 month window before SNPS builds customer adoption momentum (Cadence is the only credible competitor and their multi-die simulation capability is currently narrower and not EDA-integrated at GA status)
Ansys cost synergies ($400M target, 50% by year-end FY2026 per management) deliver on schedule -- the margin improvement validates the integration thesis and removes the exit multiple haircut
key_debates.dollar_per_share_above_base: $42/share above the $390.86 base (proportional: Ansys segment EV moves from $21.0B to $32.0B = $11.0B incremental / 0.1914B shares = $57/share; the $42 is the Daedalus-ceiling-consistent figure). The exit multiple expansion from 19.5x (base, integration risk discount) to 22.0x (bull, synergy delivery confirmed) contributes roughly one-third of this uplift; the remaining two-thirds is from higher revenue ($5.10B vs $4.25B) and higher margin (42% vs 38%).
key_debates.evidence_quality_basis: Multiphysics Fusion is GA -- the product is CONTRACTED in existence. The beta customers described as 'market leaders' are real. The addressable AI hardware design program base (TSMC CoWoS, HBM-using programs) is documented and large. The revenue contribution at scale is EXTRAPOLATED -- no customer has been named, no Multiphysics Fusion contract value disclosed, and the cross-sell motion is new organizational behavior that has not yet been tested at scale. The Ansys cost synergies are 50% CONTRACTED (management confirmed ahead of schedule); the revenue synergies are EXTRAPOLATED.
key_debates.what_changes_my_mind: Cadence announces a GA competing chip-to-system multiphysics EDA integration within 12 months -- that would remove the product uniqueness that makes the cross-sell motion possible. Alternatively: three or more quarters pass after GA with no named Multiphysics Fusion customer mentioned in earnings commentary, suggesting the cross-sell organizational challenge is proving harder than the product launch. Monitor: Ansys non-GAAP operating margin trajectory toward 38-40% blended by Q4 FY2026 (the cost synergy signal) and first Multiphysics Fusion customer naming in earnings.

clearing_price_check: YES. The Daedalus-stated bull of $624/share clears the $455.51 current price by 37.0%. Debate B1 (agentic AI, $160/share above base) alone closes approximately two-thirds of the current-price-to-bull gap ($624 - $456 = $168 required; B1 contributes $160). The bull does not require all three debates to fully materialize simultaneously -- B1 alone at half its stated impact ($80/share) plus base ($391) = $471, which clears the current price. The stock is priced for zero optionality on all three debates, which is the market's conservative reading given integration uncertainty. The path to $624 is: (1) September 30 Investor Day delivers a formal consumption pricing framework with early adoption data from the 20 evaluation customers -- this alone re-rates the stock from 26x toward 30x FY2027 EPS, implying ~$520 (30x $17.25); (2) Q1 FY2027 earnings (February 2027) shows IP QoQ growth recovery confirming royalty transition is proceeding; (3) Q2 FY2027 earnings (May 2027) shows first Multiphysics Fusion customer named and consumption revenue line item in financials. Each event is incremental. The market is not demanding all three simultaneously before moving -- it is demanding the first one (September 30 Investor Day) as the inflection trigger. That event is confirmed and dated.
self_conviction_basis: The three debates are all EXTRAPOLATED, not CONTRACTED. The product launches are real (Agent Engineer evaluations, Multiphysics Fusion GA, PCIe 7.0 launch) and the customer base is confirmed (20 evaluation customers, hyperscaler capex $725B aggregate, Samsung 2nm collaboration). What is not contracted: the dollar value of any of the three revenue streams. The consumption pricing model has no disclosed adoption rate or contract term. The royalty model transition has no disclosed royalty rate or volume commitment. Multiphysics Fusion has no named customer. A HIGH conviction bull would require at least one of these to reach CONTRACTED status -- a signed consumption pricing agreement with a named hyperscaler, or a disclosed royalty rate with a named customer. None of that evidence exists as of June 21 2026. MEDIUM is the honest conviction: the mechanism is real and evidenced, the monetization at scale is extrapolated. The bull clears the current price on extrapolated evidence, which is what a MEDIUM conviction bull should look like.
